heading: Members on active duty for 30 days or less or on inactive-duty training: separation
label: 1206 Members on active duty for 30 days or less or on inactive-duty training: separation
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)120637:272(c) (last proviso).(last proviso), .Oct. 12, 1949, ch. 681, § 402(c)63 Stat. 818To state fully in the revised section the rule contained in 37:272(c) (last proviso), the provisions of 37:272(c) (less clause (5), and less 1st proviso), and 272(f) (as applicable to 272(c)), also contained in , are repeated. The words “the member may be separated” are substituted for the words “the member concerned shall not be eligible for any disability retirement provided in this section, but may be separated for physical disability”.section 1204 of this titleClause (1) is inserted for clarity, since a member who had over 20 years of service would qualify under section 1204 or 1205 of this title.The last sentence of the revised section, relating to transfer to the inactive status list, is inserted for clarity because of .section 1209 of this title

Amendments2001—Par. (2)(B)(iii). , struck out “, if the place is outside reasonable commuting distance from the member’s residence” before semicolon at end.Pub. L. 107–107, § 513(b)Par. (5). , substituted “,” for “the date of the enactment of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2000,”.Pub. L. 107–107, § 1048(c)(6)October 5, 19991999—Par. (2). , amended par. (2) generally. Prior to amendment, par. (2) read as follows: “the disability is a result of an injury, illness, or disease incurred or aggravated in line of duty while—Pub. L. 106–65, § 578(i)(4)“(A) performing active duty or inactive-duty training;“(B) traveling directly to or from the place at which such duty is performed; or“(C) while remaining overnight immediately before the commencement of inactive-duty training, or while remaining overnight between successive periods of inactive-duty training, at or in the vicinity of the site of the inactive-duty training, if the site is outside reasonable commuting distance of the member’s residence;”.Par. (5). , inserted “, in the case of a disability incurred before the date of the enactment of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2000,” after “determination, and”.Pub. L. 106–65, § 653(c)1997—, amended section catchline generally, inserting “or on inactive-duty training” after “30 days or less”.Pub. L. 105–85, § 513(d)(2)Pars. (2) to (5). , added par. (2) and redesignated former pars. (2) to (4) as (3) to (5), respectively.Pub. L. 105–85, § 513(c)(2)1992—Par. (4). inserted before period at end “or of traveling directly to or from the place at which such duty is performed”.Pub. L. 102–4841989—Par. (4). substituted “Department of Veterans Affairs” for “Veterans’ Administration”.Pub. L. 101–1891986— struck out “; disability from injury” after “30 days or less” in section catchline and “resulting from an injury” after “because of physical disability” in provisions preceding par. (1).Pub. L. 99–661

children.text: the member has less than 20 years of service computed under ;section 1208 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: performing active duty or inactive-duty training;

children.label: ii
children.text: traveling directly to or from the place at which such duty is performed; or

children.label: iii
children.text: remaining overnight immediately before the commencement of inactive-duty training, or while remaining overnight between successive periods of inactive-duty training, at or in the vicinity of the site of the inactive-duty training, if the site is outside reasonable commuting distance of the member’s residence; or

children.label: i
children.text: was serving on funeral honors duty under or ;section 12503 of this titlesection 115 of title 32

children.label: ii
children.text: was traveling to or from the place at which the member was to so serve; or

children.label: iii
children.text: remained overnight at or in the vicinity of that place immediately before so serving;
the disability is not the result of the member’s intentional misconduct or willful neglect, and was not incurred during a period of unauthorized absence;
based upon accepted medical principles, the disability is or may be of a permanent nature; and

the disability is less than 30 percent under the standard schedule of rating disabilities in use by the Department of Veterans Affairs at the time of the determination, and, in the case of a disability incurred before , was the proximate result of performing active duty or inactive-duty training or of traveling directly to or from the place at which such duty is performed.October 5, 1999
